description: GITI-016 — `match` is a CONTEXTUAL keyword (§18.2, bryan S241 Option A). A variable named `match` (the conventional regex-result name, `const match = raw.match(/x/)`) is a legal identifier: `match is some ? match[1] : …` compiles (no misleading E-SCOPE-001 on `is`) and the regex-result path runs. A real value-return match (`match @phase { .Idle :> … }`) still lowers — the over-disambiguation guard.
rationale: Option A: `match` is reserved ONLY in the value-return / statement match-expression position `match <subject> { <arms> }`; elsewhere it is an ordinary identifier (mirrors the `to` §14.12 / `from` §21.3 contextual keywords). Clicking #go runs `const match = "id-42".match(/(\d+)/)` and `@extracted = match is some ? match[1] : "none"` (regex-result path → "42") while also advancing @phase to Done so the real derived match recomputes @label to "done".
